llcFilesWith: County clerk in each county where the business is conducted (not the Secretary of State)
soleProprietorFilesWith: County clerk in each county where the business is conducted, on the same certificate as entities
feeBasis: County clerks set their own fee, commonly about $20 to $25 per certificate; a business operating in more than one county files, and pays, in each of them.
renewalRule: A fictitious firm name certificate is effective for five years and is renewed by filing a new certificate with the county clerk.
protectionNote: A fictitious firm name is a county-level notice filing that discloses who is behind a business name; it grants no exclusive right. Because it is filed with the county clerk and not the Secretary of State, it is not part of the state entity-name distinguishability screen, so a fictitious firm name neither blocks nor is blocked by names on the Secretary of State's records.
searchNote: Fictitious firm names are held in each county clerk's records, not in the Secretary of State's business search, so a full check means searching the clerk in every county where a business operates. The Secretary of State publishes a directory of county clerk contacts for this purpose.
notes: Nevada keeps DBAs entirely at the county level. Every filer, whether an LLC, corporation, sole proprietor, or partnership, files a fictitious firm name certificate with the county clerk in each county where it does business (NRS 602.010), pays that clerk's fee (commonly about $25), and renews every five years. There is no state-level DBA filing and no newspaper-publication requirement. Because these certificates sit outside the Secretary of State's records, they do not enter the entity-name distinguishability test.
